self_report.correct_answer: ((2n-1)/e)^{(2n-1)/2} < 1·3·5···(2n-1) < ((2n+1)/e)^{(2n+1)/2} holds for all positive integers n
self_report.reflection: Setting g(t)=(t/2)ln(t/e) with g'(t)=(1/2)ln t, both bounds reduce to induction steps comparing ln(2n±1) with (1/2)∫ln t over a length-2 interval; monotonicity of ln gives strict inequalities in the right directions. Base cases at n=1 and n=2 verify by hand, and Stirling shows ln P_n sits between the two bounds with slack ~ (1/2)ln(2n).
self_report.notes: This matches the classical Putnam 1996 B2 statement; both inequalities follow by induction using strict monotonicity of ln.
